extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters filed by and against each category: I counted only the Against rows—criminal proceedings of 5 against the Company, 2 against other Directors, 10 against Promoters, and nil against KMPs/SMPs or Group Companies, totaling 17; statutory/regulatory proceedings are Nil in all Against rows, while the tax count is 14 against the Company, excluding By rows and the 2 tax matters against Group Companies because no subsidiary is identified. The quantified aggregate against the Company and its subsidiaries is the Company Against amount of ₹1,463.55 lakhs; no separate subsidiary row is disclosed, so this converts to ₹14.6355 crore at 1 crore = 100 lakhs. The contingent-liabilities figure is the stated outstanding bank guarantee of ₹67.80 lakhs as of Fiscal 2025, converted to ₹0.678 crore, and is used rather than recomputed. Customer concentration percentages are disclosed only for the Non-Retail Business segment—top five 10.30% and top ten 15.67% of revenue from operations for Fiscal 2025—so they are not company-wide and are excluded. Litigation and contingent-liability tables use lakhs as the source unit; all crore fields are converted by dividing lakh amounts by 100.
